label: faith | be | dervish
sentence:
The faith of a starving man

Once, there was a wandering dervish of great piety and faith who was starving and lost in the desert.

label: he | find | sack
sentence: As he was walking along an old pathway in search of something to eat, he found an empty fruit sack that had been thrown by the road by a previous passer-by.

label: dervish | pick | sack
sentence: The dervish picked up the sack and swung it over his shoulder while praying loudly, “Thank God for giving a starving man an empty sack of fruit,” as he continued to walk.

label: he | come | hunting
sentence: After he walked some more, he came across on old hunting bow whose string was broken.

label: he | pick | bow
sentence: He picked up the bow and put it in his sack, and prayed out loud, “Thank God for giving a starving man a hunting bow with a broken string.”

label: he | see | tree
sentence: A bit further down the road, he saw an old tree that was dead and bore no fruit.

label: he | break | branch
sentence: He broke a few dry branches off of the old tree and put them in his sack, and again said loudly, “Thank God for leading a starving man to a dead fruit tree,” and then he continued his way.

label: he | walk | ∅
sentence: He walked some more and found a dented old cooking pot.

label: he | pick | pot
sentence: He picked up the old pot off the ground, blew the dust off of it and put it in his sack too.

label: he | pray | ∅
sentence: He again loudly prayed, “Thank god for giving a starving man a dented old pot full of dust.”

label: he | find | hook
sentence: As he continued walking, he found on the ground a fishing hook but no fishing pole.

label: he | pick | hook
sentence: He picked up the fishing hook, put it in his sack and again declared loudly, “Thank God for giving a starving man a fishing hook with no fishing pole.”

label: path | end | day
sentence: Finally, after days of walking, his path ended in at a river that was so large he couldn’t see the other side.

label: dervish | fall | knee
sentence: The old dervish fell to his knees at the river bank and prayed loudly, “Thank God for leading a starving man to a river so great that he cannot hope to cross it.”

label: he | catch | fish
sentence: Then, he tied the fishing hook to the broken string on the hunting bow, and using it as a fishing pole, he caught himself a fish that he cooked in the old pot over a fire he had made from the dried tree branches.
